name: Gallagher
bio: Leo Gallagher, known simply as Gallagher, was an American prop comedian who became one of the most recognizable comic performers of the 1980s. His signature act was the Sledge-O-Matic, a large mallet he used to smash watermelons and other food, showering the front rows of his audience. A heavy touring act rather than a sitcom crossover, he played between 100 and 200 shows a year for decades and recorded numerous Showtime specials.

timeline.ev: First appearance on The Tonight Show with Johnny Carson
Films early Showtime specials An Uncensored Evening and Cosmos
Records Sledge-O-Matic specials including The Maddest
Runs in the California gubernatorial recall election

accolades: One of the most recognizable comedians of the 1980s for prop comedy
Recorded more than a dozen Showtime comedy specials
Featured in a GEICO commercial reviving the Sledge-O-Matic (2012)
